title: Unit 10: Binary Search Tree (BST)
description: A binary tree with the property: left descendants < parent < right descendants, allowing fast search and insert.

topics.title: 10.1 & 10.5 BST Search & Insertion
topics.description: Locate elements by comparing with root and recursing left/right. Insert new nodes at leaf positions.

complexity.time: O(log n) average, O(n) worst case

ispo.desc: Search accepts root pointer and target key to find.
Insert accepts root node pointer and key value to insert.
Defines Node class structure.
Allocates memory for a new Node when a null leaf slot is reached.
Branches search left or right depending on whether key is smaller or larger than root.
Recurses insert left or right and updates pointers.
Returns false if tree traversal finishes without finding the key.
Returns true if key matches root value.
Returns root node pointer to maintain recursive links.

quiz.q: What is the average time complexity of searching in a Balanced BST of size N?

topics.title: 10.2 Find Minimum Value in BST
topics.description: Find node with the minimum value. Since left descendants are smaller, the leftmost leaf contains the minimum value.

ispo.desc: Accepts BST root node reference.
Declares current pointer walking variable.
Traverses down the left child pointers until a leaf is found.
Returns the value of the leftmost node.

quiz.q: Where is the minimum value located in a Binary Search Tree?

topics.title: 10.3 Check if Binary Tree is BST
topics.description: Verify if a binary tree is a valid BST. Every node must lie within a specific min and max range value.

ispo.desc: Accepts root node of binary tree.
Validates if node value lies in bounds [min, max]. Recursively evaluates left child (with max=parent) and right child (with min=parent).
Returns true for empty subtree.
Returns false if node value violates ranges.
Returns overall logical AND result of child validations.

quiz.q: Why is check 'root.left.val < root.val < root.right.val' insufficient to validate a BST?
o: It only checks local parent-child relations but violates overall subtree level boundaries (e.g. a left descendant exceeding the root)

topics.title: 10.4 Second Largest Element in BST
topics.description: Find the second largest element in a BST. Traverse in reverse inorder (Right-Root-Left) and return the 2nd visited node.
topics.cheatSheet: Reverse Inorder: traverse right first, increment counter, check counter == 2.

ispo.desc: Takes root node pointer.
Defines helper Counter object containing iteration count and result variable.
Iterates in reverse inorder (right-subtree first). Increments counter and returns when count reaches 2.
Returns calculated second largest value.

quiz.q: If a BST has no right child for root, where is the second largest element?
o: Rightmost node of root's left subtree

topics.title: 10.6 Check BSTs Elements Equality
topics.description: Check if two Binary Search Trees (with different topologies) contain the exact same set of unique values.
topics.cheatSheet: Perform Inorder traversals on both BSTs. Compare resulting sorted arrays for equality.

ispo.desc: Takes roots root1 and root2 of both trees.
Allocates two ArrayLists to hold values of the tree nodes.
Performs inorder traversal of both trees to extract their sorted values.
Compares lists and returns boolean result.

quiz.q: Why does comparing the Inorder lists of two BSTs determine if they contain the same values?
o: Inorder traversal of a BST yields elements in sorted order; sorted arrays are equal if and only if they contain the same values in the same sequence
It is only for trees of same heights

topics.title: 10.7 Kth Largest Element in BST
topics.description: Given a BST and a positive integer k (modification not allowed), find the k'th largest element. Uses a reverse inorder traversal (Right, Root, Left) which visits nodes in descending order.

complexity.time: O(h + k) average, O(n) worst case

topics.cheatSheet:
Reverse Inorder = Right -> Root -> Left visits values largest-first.
Keep a counter; the k-th node visited is the k-th largest. Stop early once found.

ispo.desc: Accepts the BST root and integer k (the rank counted from the largest element).
Resets the shared visit counter and the result holder before traversal.
Recurses into the RIGHT subtree first so larger values are visited before smaller ones (descending order).
Increments the visit counter; when it equals k, records the current node's value and stops descending.
Returns the recorded k-th largest value.

quiz.q: How do you find the K-th largest element in a BST without modifying it?
o: Do a normal inorder traversal and take the first element
Do a reverse inorder traversal (Right, Root, Left) and stop at the K-th node visited
Use level-order traversal and count from the root down
Sort all nodes by their height in the tree
